hyperedges.label: Content Production Plan (Market → Didactics → Curriculum → Shoot)

nodes.label: NOTE: This file should not be edited
nodes.norm_label: note: this file should not be edited

nodes.label: Structural AST Extraction
nodes.rationale: Deterministic and free - code corpora need no LLM and no API key; runs in parallel with semantic extraction to save 5-15s on large corpora

nodes.label: Semantic Extraction via Parallel Subagents
nodes.rationale: Subagents dispatched in one message run in parallel (5-10x faster than reading files one-by-one); must be general-purpose type because Explore is read-only and silently drops chunk files

nodes.label: Semantic Extraction Cache
nodes.rationale: Cache check before dispatch avoids re-extracting unchanged files; stale .graphify_cached.json is deleted so Part C never merges leftovers (#1392)

nodes.label: Graph Health Check
nodes.rationale: Read-only integrity gate that surfaces edge collapse, dangling endpoints and self-loops - the silent-corruption modes of incremental updates and AST/LLM id mismatches; never aborts, but warnings must be surfaced per the Honesty Rules

nodes.label: Graph Shrink Guard
nodes.rationale: to_json refuses to write when the new graph is smaller than the existing graph.json (#479) so an empty or failed extraction never clobbers a good graph; report and analysis sidecar are only written when the graph was actually written (#1392)

nodes.label: Fast Path for Existing Graph
nodes.source_location: What You Must Do When Invoked
nodes.rationale: If graphify-out/graph.json exists and the request is a natural-language question, skip the build entirely and query - the graph is already built

nodes.label: Honesty Rules (Audit Trail)
nodes.rationale: Never invent an edge (use AMBIGUOUS when unsure), always show token cost, show raw cohesion scores - the EXTRACTED/INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS audit trail is the product's trust guarantee

nodes.label: No API Key Required Policy
nodes.rationale: graphify never blocks on an API key: code is extracted via AST with no LLM; semantic extraction uses Gemini only if a key is already set, otherwise the host agent itself is the LLM. Prompting for ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is defined as a misread of the skill

nodes.label: Discrete Confidence Score Rubric
nodes.rationale: Models follow discrete rubrics better than continuous ranges; production showed a bimodal collapse (>50% at 0.5, >40% at 0.85+) so the range was replaced with five fixed values and 0.5 was banned as a default

nodes.label: Full-Path Node ID Format
nodes.rationale: IDs use the full repo-relative path so same-named files in different directories stay distinct and semantic IDs match AST-generated IDs; filename-only or immediate-parent IDs create orphan ghost-duplicate nodes

nodes.label: Constrained Query Expansion
nodes.rationale: The query CLI matches via case-folded substring + IDF with no stemming or synonyms, so vocabulary mismatch returns 0 hits; expanding only with tokens from the graph's own vocabulary fixes this without inventing tokens

nodes.label: BFS/DFS Graph Traversal
nodes.rationale: BFS for broad context (what is X connected to), DFS depth-limited to 6 for tracing a specific chain

nodes.label: save-result Feedback Loop
nodes.rationale: Answers are saved back into the graph so the next --update extracts each Q&A as a node, improving future queries

nodes.label: Work Memory (reflect / LESSONS.md)
nodes.rationale: Outcome tags (useful/dead_end/corrected) feed graphify reflect, which maintains LESSONS.md listing preferred sources, known dead ends and corrections for future sessions

nodes.label: Incremental Update (--update)
nodes.rationale: Only re-extracts new/changed files to save tokens; code-only changes skip semantic extraction entirely; prune_sources is only for genuinely deleted files because build_merge's replace-on-re-extract reconciles changed files (#1344, #1178)

nodes.label: build_merge Graph Merge
nodes.rationale: Reads graph.json directly without a NetworkX round-trip so edge direction is always preserved (#801); root= relativizes prune paths so stale nodes do not accumulate (#1361)

nodes.label: Cluster-Only Rerun
nodes.rationale: Self-contained CLI command; Steps 5-9 must not be re-run because they read intermediate files already deleted by a prior cleanup (#1392)

nodes.label: Whisper Video/Audio Transcription
nodes.rationale: God-node labels are turned into a one-sentence domain hint passed as the Whisper initial prompt - the host agent composes it, no separate API call; transcripts are then treated as doc files

nodes.label: Watch Mode Auto-Rebuild
nodes.rationale: Code-only changes rebuild immediately with no LLM; doc/paper/image changes only set a needs_update flag because they require LLM re-extraction; debounce prevents one rebuild per file during agent write waves

nodes.label: Neo4j / FalkorDB Cypher Export
nodes.rationale: Uses MERGE so re-runs never create duplicates; FalkorDB prefers direct push because GRAPH.QUERY runs one statement at a time with no bulk script import

nodes.label: Token Reduction Benchmark
nodes.rationale: Only run when total_words > 5000 - for small corpora the graph's value is structural clarity, not token compression

nodes.label: Cross-Repo Graph Merge
nodes.rationale: Monorepo subfolders use the CLI directly (graphify-out inside each scanned path) because the skill pipeline would clobber one shared output dir; merged nodes carry a repo attribute for filtering

nodes.label: Post-Commit Auto-Rebuild Hook
nodes.rationale: Triggers once per commit via git diff HEAD~1 - no background process needed; doc/image changes are ignored and need a manual --update

nodes.label: AI Academy Platform
nodes.rationale: Not 'another AI course' but an ecosystem making learners measurably more productive within weeks; German-language sub-product of MadkourMedia under /academy

nodes.label: Tech Stack (Next.js/Supabase/Stripe/Mux/Vercel)
nodes.rationale: Supabase chosen for Auth+Storage+Realtime+RLS in one; Mux for signed adaptive streaming; Stripe for subscriptions; Vercel for edge deploys and ISR

nodes.label: localStorage Progress Store (store.ts)
nodes.rationale: Progress (XP, streaks, badges, certificates, plan) runs on a localStorage store with the same API Supabase will serve later - migrating the backend means only swapping load/persist

nodes.label: RBAC Role Model
nodes.rationale: guest→student→pro→instructor→admin→super_admin, enforced at three layers (Supabase RLS at DB level, middleware guards at route level, UI gating) - never client-side only

nodes.label: Academy Domain Model (Level→Chapter→Lesson)
nodes.rationale: Types in src/lib/academy/types.ts are the Single Source of Truth; Academy→Level→Chapter→Lesson with Prompts, Resources, Quiz, plus User Progress and Prompt/Tool libraries

nodes.label: Clean-Architecture-Light Layering
nodes.rationale: UI → feature components → domain types → services; the service serves mock data today and Supabase later while the UI stays unchanged

nodes.label: Market Analysis 2026 (Demand Ranking)
nodes.rationale: Demand order: AI agents & workflow automation first (Gartner: ~40% of enterprise apps with task-specific agents by end of 2026, most new roles no-code), then workflow design, prompting, context engineering, AI judgment - so course weight must shift from 'understand AI' to 'workflows & agents' where the paid tier begins

nodes.label: OpenAI Academy Benchmark
nodes.rationale: Strongest free competitor; its weaknesses define the differentiation: German/DACH examples, tool-agnostic (ChatGPT and Claude/Gemini/Perplexity/n8n), a real person, community, pass-based certificates with score, industry personas

nodes.label: 7-Point Didactics Checklist
nodes.rationale: Outcome over topic, one artifact per lesson, quick win in minute 1 (decides completion rates), 5-12 min single-skill videos, practice over watching, built-in repetition, honesty about limits (builds trust ad-driven courses never get)

nodes.label: Target Curriculum (3 Levels, 12 Lessons)
nodes.rationale: Level 3 renamed from 'AI Master' to 'AI Workflows & Agenten' and made the pro-tier focus because that is where demand and willingness to pay converge; the old content had 4 basics lessons but only 1 agents lesson - the inverse of demand

nodes.label: Hybrid Production (Face Front, AI Backstage)
nodes.rationale: No AI avatars as the core: avatar videos are 2026 commodity nobody trusts and would destroy the strongest differentiator (a real DACH person showing how they work); AI handles scripts, shotlists, graphics, quizzes, subtitles while the human films one batch day per level - 20-30% talking head, 60-70% screen recording, 10% graphics

nodes.label: Visitor Funnel Strategy
nodes.rationale: Landing sells transformation not features; Level 1 fully free as honest trial; prompt library .md export as lead magnet against newsletter; shorts from screen recordings as traffic source

nodes.label: Context Engineering
nodes.rationale: Giving agents the right information (knowledge, tools, limits) rather than just the right question - replacing prompt engineering as the differentiator

nodes.label: Prompt Library (14+ Prompts, .md Export)
nodes.rationale: 6 prompts felt empty; expanded to 14+ including agent/workflow prompts, exportable as .md for Obsidian/Notion users and reused as the lead magnet

nodes.label: Verifiable Certificate Issuance
nodes.rationale: Issued on quiz pass with code + name + QR + LinkedIn link - a pass-based certificate with score, deliberately stronger than OpenAI Academy's participation certificate

nodes.label: Warm Editorial Design System
nodes.rationale: Deliberately away from the 'AI look': paper-light/espresso-dark, vermilion accent, ink buttons with hard offset shadows, film grain, marker highlights instead of gradient text, Syne poster typography - all tokens under .academy in globals.css

nodes.label: Mobile Display Root-Cause Fix (.ac-btn vs hidden)
nodes.rationale: .ac-btn/.ac-pill set display after Tailwind utilities and beat 'hidden', which caused a 131px overflow on every page - never combine hidden with these classes, hide a wrapper instead; verified 0px horizontal overflow at 390px via Playwright
nodes.norm_label: mobile display root-cause fix (.ac-btn vs hidden)

nodes.label: Open Items (Next Session Order)
nodes.rationale: Ordered: video production first (Level 1 shoot day), real downloads, Supabase schema+RLS, Stripe, Mux/Bunny video hosting, lead-magnet funnel, industry tracks (real estate first for JP DL AG synergy)

nodes.label: User Preferences (Monther)
nodes.rationale: German Du-form, reviews on phone, wants 'loop mode' (Claude works end-to-end autonomously then reports compactly), 'Merge' command = PR + merge to main (Vercel live), design must not look AI-generated

links.source_location: For the commit hook and native CLAUDE.md integration

hyperedges.label: Content Production Plan (Market → Didactics → Curriculum → Shoot)
